entry.title: Re: Integration C4C --> S4: How to block a contact person...
entry.content:
Hi @Christian77,

Yes, that is a very common and frustrating edge case when dealing with open reference documents! You are absolutely right—if the new Sales Order copies the partner from an open contract, and the relationship has been expired, S/4HANA will pull the partner number into VBPA but leave the name fields blank due to the deleted relation. If that document tries to replicate back, it will almost certainly cause an error in C4C because the system recognizes the party ID is blocked.

To handle this properly, the best practice is to stop the invalid contact at the source (during the Sales Order creation in S/4HANA) rather than trying to manage replication failures downstream. Here are the standard ways to resolve this:

1. Intervene via SD Copy Control (Recommended) You need to prevent the blocked contact from copying over from the contract to the new sales order in the first place.

  • VOFM Data Transfer Routine: You can implement a custom routine in Copy Control (transaction VTAA for Contract to Order). When copying partner data, the routine should check the validity dates of the contact in table BUT050. If the relationship is expired or invalid today, the routine should simply clear that partner line from the new order.

  • Incompletion Log: Combine this with an SD incompletion procedure. If your business process requires a Contact Person on the order, and the old one was wiped by the routine, the order becomes incomplete. The user cannot save it until they manually select a new, active contact person.

2. User Exit / BAdI Validation Alternatively, you can force a validation check right before the document saves. By utilizing USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE (in program MV45AFZZ) or the equivalent S/4HANA BAdI, the system can check the partner's validity. If the contact is expired, throw a standard warning or error (e.g., "Contact Person is no longer valid. Please select a new active contact."). This forces the CSR to clean up the data before the order is ever saved.

3. Middleware (CPI/PI) Filtering as a Safety Net While the ERP side should be fixed, you can also add a safeguard in your middleware. You can configure your CPI mapping so that if an outbound sales order payload contains a Contact Person ID but the name fields are completely blank (or it queries the status and sees it's blocked), CPI simply strips that specific partner node from the payload before it ever hits C4C.

Implementing the validation in S/4HANA (Option 1 or 2) is the cleanest approach because it keeps your ERP documents consistent and forces users to update the contact immediately.

Let me know if you need any specific details on the user exits to get this working!

entry.title: Formal Name Not Just a Field
entry.content:
What is the Name Format feature?

The Name Format is a feature that concatenates name fields based on your specific configuration. Additionally, it allows you to define different concatenation rules for each legal entity.

For example, you can set a format like First Name + Preferred Name + Last Name specifically for a Legal Entity in Germany, while using Last Name + First Name for one in Japan.

How do I enable the Name Format feature?

To enable this, you must select "Enable Name Format" under "Company Settings" in Provisioning.

 

How is the concatenation configured?

The concatenation logic is defined within the Name Format object. (Manage Data) 

How do I assign a Name Format to a company?

You assign the Name Format through the Legal Entity object.

If you don't see the "Name Format for Legal Entity" is because you have not implemented the feature yet. You hace to associate the object to the "Legal Entity" object KBA.

Where is the concatenation displayed?

Once all the configuration is complete, the formal-name field (personal-info) is the one that will reflect the name concatenation you have defined. This ensures that the system automatically populates the name in the specific format required by your company.

 

Where will I see the Formal Name concatenation?

The configured format directly impacts the Formal Name field within the personalInfo entity. Furthermore, this is the field that populates the information displayed on Quickcards.

Does this Name Format affect the "General Display Name"?

Yes. The General Display Name (GDN) is an SAP feature designed to show employee names consistently across the entire SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite. Once the "Name Format" (in manage data) is configured, that specific concatenation is what will be reflected in the GDN feature (additional configuration is required).  For more detailed information, you can refer to the official SAP blog post: Adopting General Display Name.

In Summary

entry.title: Re: YCOA Account Groups - Verification of Usage and Diffe...
entry.content:
Hello Byd,

Please accept the solution and close the thread if you are satisfied by the response.

Account Group Correct Understanding / Clarification

ABST✔ Correct. Standard reconciliation accounts for AR/AP. Direct postings are NOT allowed. Used strictly for subledger integration (customer/vendor).
ANL.✔ Correct. Used for Asset Accounting balance sheet accounts (APC, accumulated depreciation).
ERG.✔ Correct. P&L accounts (revenues, expenses).
FIN.✔ Correct. Bank, cash, liquidity accounts.
MAT.✔ Correct. Inventory and MM-related accounts (BSX, WRX, etc.).
RECN⚠ Partially correct. These are special reconciliation accounts that allow direct postings (used in specific scenarios like Alternative Reconciliation Accounts).
SAKO✔ Correct. General-purpose G/L accounts (non-specialized balance sheet or P&L).
SASL✔ Correct. Used for ledger-specific postings (rare, advanced scenarios).
SECC

✔ Correct. Secondary cost elements (CO allocations, assessments, distributions).

entry.title: Re: REVERSING GOOD RECEIPT AND OPEN PURCHASE ORDERS FOR P...
entry.content:
Dear @yinka_o,

In Public Cloud there is no single standard mass reversal function that automatically reverses all related open purchase orders, goods receipts and goods issues for a list of products in order to change the valuation class

SAP confirms that the valuation class cannot be changed if any of the following exist for the material:

  1. valuated stock in the current or previous period
  2. open purchase orders or delivery schedule lines
  3. production orders for which a goods movement has already taken place

The first step should therefore be to open the material in Change Material / MM02 try to change the valuation class and use Display Errors. For PO-related blockers, SAP states that the system can show the exact purchase orders causing the issue

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3712400

For open purchase orders the correct action is not a header deletion. In Public Cloud the delete function in Manage Purchase Order applies to draft documents only for real POs the relevant action is the deletion indicator at item level. SAP also states that Mass PO can be used to set the deletion indicator in bulk. The Mass Changes to Purchase Orders app is the standard app for changing multiple purchase orders simultaneously

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002526306

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/cdf07a48244744bda4635d154599e5d7.html

For goods receipts and goods issues the documents should be analyzed and reversed only where this is technically possible, business-correct, and required to remove the valuation-class blocker. SAP provides standard cancellation/reversal options for goods movements and the Material Document API also supports cancellation operations but this is still document-based and subject to normal SAP checks. It is not a generic reverse all transactions for these products mass tool

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/1aef4e402acd4c8b8ec2ea2bfda7715b.html

Summary:

There is no standard Public Cloud tool to bulk-reverse all PO/GR/GI history just to change valuation class. The standard route is to remove the exact blockers first. For large historical cases, creating new product numbers with the correct valuation class and phasing out the old products is often the safer business solution

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Is class_constructor a good method to place the buffe...
entry.content:
Hello,

depends on when you need the buffered data. If the data is used when instantiating the class objects, use the class constructor.
Otherwise, I would suggest a lazy approach. Read the table/fill the static buffer only if a class instance method is used that actually requires the data. Because else you potentially waste memory and runtime if a class instance is created.

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Events for Oil, Gas, and Energy 2025/2026
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal! É um prazer fazer parte desta comunidade SAP.

Meu nome é Criste Jones, sou de Mossoró/RN, cidade com forte tradição no setor de petróleo e gás, e atuo há mais de duas décadas na indústria de energia, com experiência em operações, manutenção e gestão, especialmente em estações de atualizações de gás natural e processos operacionais do segmento de petróleo e gás.

Ao longo da minha trajetória, acompanhei de perto os desafios e oportunidades de integração entre tecnologia, eficiência operacional e gestão empresarial, e por isso vejo no SAP uma ferramenta estratégica para transformar processos, aumentar a produtividade e apoiar decisões no setor energético.

Também sou autor de livros voltados para a área, compartilhando conhecimento prático e técnico com profissionais, estudantes e empresas. Acredito muito no poder da troca de experiências e do aprendizado contínuo.

Estou aqui para aprender com todos vocês, contribuir com minha experiência no segmento de petróleo e gás e ampliar conexões com profissionais que enxergam a tecnologia como motor de crescimento e inovação.

Contem comigo!

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 Analytics - April 2026
entry.content:
Thanks @RACHID_AITBOUALE I'm glad you liked the blog. We currently do not support the OData protocol for extracting data from SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and SAP Enterprise Service Management solutions. Only REST & Event APIs are currently supported, & extension field data is part of those APIs as well.

Analytics is actually a bit late to the extension where-used list 'party' 🙂 The list already contains details of any SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and SAP Enterprise Service Management UI(s) in which an extension field is used. Since it would need to be placed in a standard UI for customers to be able to enter data, that dependency should be enough for Admins to take decisions when performing any cleanup/optimization.

entry.title: Soldoc- Cloud ALM Migration
entry.content:
Hi Team,
We are planning to migrate soldoc objects especially documents to  Cloud ALM. We were exploring different options and we found DMS can be integrated with cloud ALM for storing documents. Below are the key points need to check.

  1. How to find the current size of soldoc in solman
  2. What is the best approach to migrate the documents to cloud  ALM

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Soldoc- Cloud ALM Migration
entry.content:
Hello
1) The documents in a 7.2 system are stored in table SMDCONT. This table contains the PHIO_ID (versions) of the documents.
Every document has a unique LIOI_ID that can be found in the table SMDLOIO. The PHIO's are stored in the table SMDPHIO.
If you want to check the file size of a specific document, you need to have the PHIO ID - with this ID you can check the table SMDPHF. There you find the File size (in bytes).

Alternative you can check the table size of SMDCONT (contains all versions of the documents).

2) Please refer to Selective Data Transfer from: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/selective_data_transfer_to_calm.html
specially End To End Guide:

End-to-End-Guide

section: 6.5 Transferring Documents

Best regards
SAP Support

Specially the End to End Guide:

End-to-End-Guide
6.5 Transferring Documents

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Payments with Postal Money Order to Account (Incoming Payments) for SAP S/4HANA incl. Private Cloud
entry.content:
This blog post introduces a special incoming payment - money order through the post offices in Slovakia and Czech Republic for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private edition.

When your customer pays you through the post office, you can get the money either in cash at the post office or receive it in your bank accounts according to your local regulations. You process the file from the post office as an incoming payment.

In this blog post the focus is the business scenarios with postal services in Czech Republic and Slovakia. When the payment is paid by the customers at the post office, you get the file from the post office. You then import and process this incoming payment file.
For the specific payment formats for such services - Postal Money Order to Account:

  • Czech Republic - CZ-MONEYORDER-A
  • Slovakia -  SK-MONEYORDER-U

For other business scenarios of third-party payment, see the following blog post:

  • Postal Payment with Third Party (Outgoing Payments) - SAP S/4HANA incl. Cloud Private Edition

Prerequisite

Define Payment Lot Specifications

Open the Define Payment Lot Specifications activity using the view V_TFKIDEXPC_ZK.
Select the format type based on the country and complete the Bank statement settings section.

  • Czech Republic - CZ-MONEYORDER-A
  • Slovakia -  SK-MONEYORDER-U

Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions

Open the Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions activity using the view TFK012D to fill in the Customer number.

Maintain number ranges for Convergent Invoicing

Open the Maintain number ranges for invoicing activity using the transaction FKKINVDOC_NUM to set up the number ranges.

Invoice numbers in Convergent Invoicing, namely external reference number, are considered as a variable symbol in the payment processing to identify the invoices. This variable number can only be up to 10 characters. Therefore, you can configure the number length and its range based on your business requirements.

For example, you create a number range 91 as follows.

Maintain Document Types for Invoicing Documents and Assign Number Ranges

This activity is a continuous activity to the previous step.
Open the Maintain Document Types for Invoicing Documents and Assign Number Ranges activity using the view cluster VC_TFK2600_INVO in the transaction SM34.
Select a document type and assign the number range number that you have configured in the previous step.

Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee 

Open the Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee activity using the view cluster VC_TFK006 in the transaction SM34 to assign a reference document number.

Procedure of Generating Payment with Postal Money Orders

  1. Set up the master data of the business partner with address (without bank connection).
    Create a contract account for this business partner.
  2. Post a receivable item in Contract Accounting or in Convergent Invoicing that your customer can pay through the Czech Post.
  3. The customer receives payment instructions, or correspondence, with the payment details for paying through the Post.
    1. In Czech Republic, the payer pays the invoice amount in cash using the Money Order A form (poštovní poukázka A) through the Czech Post.
      The Czech Post then collects the payments and provides the file listing all paid money orders to your system. The payment with the total amount is transferred to the payee’s bank account.
    2. In Slovakia, the payer pays the invoice in cash using money order U form (Poštový poukaz U) at the Slovak Post. The Post transfers the money in one of the following ways:
      1. Collect the payments and send the file listing all paid money orders to your banking system, and the total payment to your bank account.
      2.  Send the data file with a list of money orders to you. The file indicates which invoices are paid.
  4. Import the files sent by the bank. Open the Import Files for Third-Party Payments using the transaction IDFIEXPC_IMPORT.
    Select a format type and specify the file path.
  5. If you use SAP Fiori Launchpad, search for the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680).
    • Select Import and choose Third-party Payment.
    • Select a format (SK: Slovak Republic: Money Order U; CZ: Czech Republic: Money Order A), upload the file, and process.

  6. After the import of the payment file, process the imported file in the Process Imported Third-Party Payments using the transaction IDFIEXPC_TRANSFER.
    • Select the Format Type.
    • You can select the Post Lot checkbox to directly process the payment order lot.
    • Execute and you an see the payment lot is created in the log details.

Is this blog post helpful to you? Leave your comments to share with us your thoughts.

entry.title: Lease-in Contract Purchase Option at the end of the lease contract
entry.content:
SAP,

We have a lease scenario wherein the contract agreement with the supplier has a purchase option at the end of the lease contract. We can't find any SAP documentation on within the SAP KBA datamine, SAP community, or anywhere else that gives us the instructions on how to add this condition type to the lease contract.

Example: The contract starts on 5/22/2025 and ends on 4/30/2026 with a monthly lease payment of $1000. We have all the contract details added, including the rhythm rule and condition rule for the monthly lease amount; however, the contract with the supplier has a purchase option of $5,000 at the end of the contract. We don't see a condition type for this option, nor do we fully understand the logic behind the lease condition types.

We need to know how to add the purchase option to the contract and understand what each condition type means. Again, we can't find any details for this scenario.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Lease-in Contract Purchase Option at the end of the l...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

Purchase options can be maintained on lease contracts via the pre-delivered LG30 - Surrender value condition type. With this condition, the default valuation property will be set as 'D - Cash Surrender Value' which will indicate a purchase option at the end of the contract term.

You can read more about the calculation logic and the exact steps to utilize in KBA 2912873 - Valuation: How to handle purchase option and remaining value guarantee - SAP for Me, scenario 1 - Purchase Option. This outlines the standard system logic as well the necessary additional steps to consider for the valuation parameters as well.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud: Best practices for SAP Business Warehouse modernization miniseries
entry.content:
Modernizing SAP BW can feel like a big step.  The good news: you don’t have to figure it out alone.

Join our 3-episode miniseries, “SAP Business Data Cloud: Best practices for SAP Business Warehouse modernization,” built for SAP Partners who want clear, practical guidance.  We walk through what works in real projects, not theory.

We start with readiness and planning for Private Cloud Edition.  Think sizing, roadmaps, and how to keep interfaces stable.  Then we move into SAP Datasphere.  You’ll see how to design scalable models and manage governance at enterprise level.  Finally, we cover Data Product Generator.  This is where reusable data products and mixed scenarios come together.

Plus, you’ll see real customer examples and a hands-on demo in the Technical Academy.

Attend all three and earn a digital badge that signals your expertise.

entry.title: What's New in 2605, Release Navigator for Spend Management
entry.content:
Q2 2026 is here and with a new quarter, comes new innovation! The 2605 updates for the release navigator are live; this update is primarily focused on content maintenance. 

SAP Ariba Procurement and SAP Business Network Quarterly Releases are scheduled for the weekend May 15th, 2026. Make sure to go to the Release Navigator for Spend Management to find the most recent Webcasts, Feature Lists, Tutorial Videos, Blogs, and more!

Tool Overview

The Release Navigator for Spend Management is a tool provided by SAP Enterprise Support designed to be your central hub for understanding and managing new software releases for SAP Ariba, SAP Business Network, SAP Fieldglass, & SAP Concur. It allows you to easily locate essential resources for upcoming releases and quickly find cross-solution materials, documentation, webcasts, and more all in one tool!

2605 Feature Release Highlights for SAP Ariba & Business Network:

  • Enhancements to the SAP Integration Suite, Managed Gateway for Spend Management and SAP Business Network Add-On [NGPROCINT-452]
  • UI Updates Across SAP Ariba Category Management Apps [ARBCATA-607]
  • Additional Authorizations for AI Copilot Joule Scenarios in Supplier Management [SLP-2412]

Don’t forget to register for the 2605 Release Webcasts here.

Innovation Schedule

To ensure a smooth user experience, the Release Navigator introduces new user‑experience updates once per year in August, alongside refreshed release content.

The Release Navigator will update automatically over the coming weeks—no action is required on your end.

entry.title: Restart Failed E-Signatures Without Restarting the Entire Onboarding Process post 1H 2026 Release
entry.content:
Introduction:
Your new hire's onboarding is 90% complete, then the e-signature step fails or incorrect information was pre-populated to the form. Until now, you had to restart the entire onboarding process. Starting with the 1H 2026 release, you can now restart just the e-signature step without touching anything else.

How is this feature helpful?

  • Better New Hire Experience: Re-sign specific documents only, not redo completed tasks
  • Targeted Resolution: Works for External User Documents and Internal Hire Documents

Configuration & Prerequisites:

  1. Grant "Restart Onboarding for E-Signature" under Onboarding or Offboarding Permissions
  2. Configure "(ONB) Onboarding Task Restart Trigger" Email

NOTE: This feature is applicable for DocuSign and SuccessFactors E-Signature.

How to Use It?

Use Case #1:  Incorrect Data in custom forms.

Scenario: Candidates E-Signature forms have incorrect pre-populated information in the custom forms.

Steps:

  1. Onboarding Dashboard → Locate candidate's record → Click on New Hire Data
  2. Correct the necessary information associated with the candidate so that the forms can be updated successfully
  3. Go to the onboarding dashboard, locate the candidate → click on View Details for E-Signature Task → Click "Restart e-Signature"
  4. Select document (e.g., "Offer Letter") → Enter reason → Confirm

Result: Fresh custom forms are sent within minutes. Completed tasks remain untouched.

Use Case #2: Custom Forms were accidentally skipped for the candidate

Scenario: Post completion of onboarding steps, the rule with scenario "Assign Form" did not evaluate to true for the impacted candidate causing the custom PDF forms to skip for the candidate.

Steps:

  1. Onboarding Dashboard → Locate candidate's record → Click on New Hire Data
  2. Correct the necessary information associated with the candidate so that the rule with scenario "Assign Form" can be evaluated to true successfully
  3. Go to the onboarding dashboard, locate the candidate → click on View Details for E-Signature Task → Click "Restart e-Signature"
  4. Select document → Enter reason → Confirm

Use Case #3: Custom PDF Forms are assigned but the fields within the form are empty.

Scenario: Candidate is successfully assigned a form but was unable to see a few field values within the form. Either the candidate has reported at this stage or after the submission of E-Signature Step.

Steps:

  1. Validate the data passed via mapping in Maintain Onboarding or Offboarding Document Template. This can be done by creating a new object named "Document Template Mapping Preview" in Manage Data. 
  2. With the help of its results, you can maintain the necessary field values based on the placeholder name
  3. Correct the necessary information associated with the candidate based on placeholder mapping
  4. Go to the onboarding dashboard, locate the candidate → click on View Details for E-Signature Task → Click "Restart e-Signature"
  5. Select document → Enter reason → Confirm

entry.title: Re: SAP Document Reporting and Compliance Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Dear @archaradhakrishnan,

In Public Cloud scope item 5XU SAP Document and Reporting Compliance provides the S/4HANA-side DRC capabilities, including electronic document processing and statutory reporting features. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition is a separate cloud service used as the external communication/submission layer for example to exchange electronic documents with tax authorities, Peppol/access points or other external communication parties. For scenarios requiring this integration, SAP uses scope item 78L SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition Integration.

The eDocuments created from S/4HANA business documents are not available only in BTP Cockpit. They are still processed and monitored in the S/4HANA apps, especially eDocument Cockpit and/or Manage Electronic Documents, depending on the country and scenario. SAP explicitly states that eDocument Cockpit is used to process eDocuments created automatically from source documents. The cloud edition supports the external exchange/submission and can hold related communication, routing, job and message information but it does not replace the S/4HANA eDocument Cockpit for S/4-generated eDocuments

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e2d057b7b4df44ba941a040d4dda2956/d43668ab46b1406c8b3f4a187f7086bc.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/what-is-sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f2dbf96a245c44809852a5465ed7e93d/df272aab94064e16a5a2c9d462d81cc7.html

Summary:

Use 5XU for the S/4HANA DRC foundation. Use DRC cloud edition / 78L where the legal scenario requires cloud-based external exchange or submission. The business eDocument record remains visible/processable in S/4HANA via eDocument Cockpit / Manage Electronic Documents BTP/cloud edition supports the communication and integration layer

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Warehouse Logistics: April 2026 Feature Highlights
entry.content:
On April 20, we released a new set of features for the SAP Logistics Management mobile App - SAP Warehouse Logistics, focusing on improving usability, increasing flexibility in warehouse execution, and enabling more efficient day-to-day operations. 

Summary of Key Innovations 

This release introduces several enhancements designed to streamline warehouse processes and improve the user experience: 

  • New Home Screen for faster and more intuitive navigation 
  • Putaway Task Handling, enabling seamless task processing on the go 
  • Product, Bin, and HU Lookup for quick access to warehouse information 
  • Ad Hoc Warehouse Tasks to handle unplanned work more efficiently 
  • Localized Units of Measure, displayed in the user’s selected language 

Together, these updates help users work more efficiently, respond to changes faster, and reduce operational complexity. 

New Home Screen Experience 

With this release, we introduce a completely redesigned home screen to streamline navigation and improve the overall user experience. 

Home Screen

Key improvements include: 

  • Simplified and intuitive layout, allowing users to quickly access core warehouse functions 
  • Faster navigation, reducing the number of steps required to start common tasks 
  • Improved visual structure, helping users focus on what matters most 

The new home screen ensures that users can begin their work more efficiently right from the moment they open the app. 

Putaway Tasks Handling 

Warehouse operatives can now execute putaway tasks directly within the app, enabling a more seamless and guided workflow. 

With this release, we introduce a new entry point “Putaway” in the Quick Access area, making it easier to find and process relevant tasks. 

This enhancement allows users to: 

  • Access putaway tasks quickly via the new Quick Access entry point 
  • Enter or scan a product or storage bin to identify associated putaway tasks 
  • Select and review task details in a dedicated task screen before execution 
  • Start and confirm tasks seamlessly, similar to the picking process 
  • Verify destinations using manual input or barcode scanning, ensuring accuracy during execution 

During task processing, users also have additional flexibility: 

  • Adjust the quantity if required during execution 
  • Change the destination storage bin directly within the task screen

Putaway Direct Entry     Search for Product or Bin     Putaway Task List     Putaway Task Details     Putaway Task Processing     Confirm Putaway Task

In addition, users benefit from multiple ways to locate putaway tasks: 

  • Queue-based processing for structured task execution 
  • Search by delivery for inbound-related tasks 
  • Direct lookup via specific warehouse task numbers 

By combining flexible search options with guided execution and verification, this feature significantly improves efficiency and accuracy in putaway processing while aligning with familiar picking workflows.

Product, Bin, and Handling Unit Lookup 

A new lookup capability makes it easier to access key warehouse information in real time, directly within the app. 

Users can now enter or scan a product, storage bin, or handling unit (HU) to quickly retrieve relevant information and improve decision-making during operations. 

Depending on the object, users gain different insights: 

  • Product lookup
    • View key product attributes such as weight and dimensions
    • Check the current stock situation 
    • Identify where the product is stored within the warehouse
  • Storage bin lookup
    • See what is currently stored in a bin, including products or handling units 
    • Gain better visibility into bin utilization and contents
  • Handling unit (HU) lookup
    • Identify the current location of the HU 
    • View what materials or products are contained within the HU 

Lookup Result for Product     Lookup Result for Bin     Lookup Result for HU

This functionality provides full transparency across warehouse objects and reduces the need to switch between systems or rely on manual checks, enabling faster and more informed execution of warehouse tasks.

Ad Hoc Warehouse Task Processing 

To better handle unexpected situations, the app now supports receiving and performing ad hoc warehouse tasks. 

With this feature, users can: 

  • Search for ad hoc warehouse tasks directly within the app 
  • Select and execute tasks on demand, without prior planning 
  • Quickly respond to unplanned work, ensuring operational continuity 

During task processing, the process is guided to ensure accuracy: 

  • Source, product, and destination must be verified 
  • Verification can be performed via manual input or barcode scanning, following established warehouse execution patterns 

Search for Ad Hoc Task     Process Ad Hoc Task

This ensures that even unplanned tasks are carried out with the same level of control and reliability as standard warehouse processes, improving both flexibility and execution quality. 

Commercial Units of Measure in User Language 

The app now displays commercial units of measure for products in the language selected in user settings. 

Benefits include: 

  • Improved clarity for international users 
  • Reduced risk of misinterpretation 
  • More user-friendly and localized experience 

English UoM     German UoM

This enhancement supports global warehouse teams by making product information easier to understand and use. 

Conclusion 

The April 20 release focuses on enhancing usability, flexibility, and real-time access to information within the SAP Warehouse Logistics App. These innovations help warehouse operatives work smarter, faster, and with greater confidence in their daily operations. 

We hope these new features help improve your daily warehouse operations and enhance the overall user experience. 

If you have any feedback or questions, feel free to share them in the comments section - we’d be happy to hear from you.

SAP Logistics Management

entry.title: Re: TO print checkbox, need to be turned off for SLOC-Lev...
entry.content:
Hi @PRANALI6 ,
My friend in SAP WM, the checkbox that controls whether a Transfer Order (TO) prints automatically is managed in Print Control settings, not at the SLOC level. To “turn off” TO printing, you must adjust the WM movement type or print code configuration.

Turn off TO printing via WM Movement Type (most common) Go to SPRO
Logistics Execution → Warehouse Management → Activities → Transfers → Print → Define Print Control
Find the Print Code used for your WM movement type. In the Print Control settings, Set Print Indicator = 0 (No printing) Or remove the assigned Print Code from the WM movement type. Then Save.

entry.title: Everything you need to achieve Enterprise-Wide Integration and iPaaS Consolidation with SAP
entry.content:
1. Introduction – Why Integration Platform Sprawl Is Holding You Back

As enterprises accelerate their cloud journeys, integration has become both more critical—and more complex - than ever. At the same time, AI is transforming how businesses operate, intensifying the need for seamless, real-time connectivity across systems. Yet many organizations face a hidden barrier: integration platform sprawl.

A recent survey done by SAP and the Americas’ SAP Users’ Group (ASUG), with more than 500 respondents, reveals the scale of the challenge. The average enterprise manages around 18 applications requiring integration, with roughly 30 integration points per application. That translates to nearly 600 individual integrations per organization. Compounding the issue, 75% of respondents report using three or more integration tools simultaneously, as shown in a recent study by IDC.

The result is a fragmented and costly integration landscape that is difficult to manage, slows innovation, and increases exposure to security and compliance risks. There is, however, a clear path forward - and it starts with simplifying and unifying integration. SAP Integration Suite plays a central role in enabling that transformation.

 

2. The Reality of Integration Platform Sprawl

Most organizations did not end up here by accident. Each integration platform in today's landscape is usually there for a good reason - a company acquisition, a business unit that wanted its own tooling, or an ongoing migration. In isolation, each of those decisions made sense in retrospect.

The challenge is the cumulative effect:

  • Fragmented visibility: When something breaks, teams need deep institutional knowledge just to know which platform to log into. Troubleshooting becomes slow and expensive.
  • Slowed innovation: Deciding which tool to use for a new requirement can turn into a cross-departmental negotiation - delaying projects before they even start.
  • Security and compliance risk: Every additional platform is another attack vector and another audit burden.
  • Higher total cost of ownership: Licensing, personnel, and operational overhead multiply with every platform - and skilled integration professionals end up managing infrastructure instead of delivering value.

These are not even abstract risks. They translate directly into costs, delays, and missed opportunities.

 

3. The Hidden Costs of Doing Nothing

The challenges above tend to compound over time. Many organizations find themselves sitting on significant "integration debt" - a backlog of undocumented, poorly understood interfaces that carry both technical and business risk.

At the same time, the integration tools many enterprises rely on today were built for a different era - one of batch processing, file transfers, and mostly on-premise architectures. Today's reality looks very different:

  • Applications span on-premise environments, hyperscalers, and SaaS platforms all at once.
  • Business models change in weeks, not years.
  • AI agents are starting to interact with enterprise systems in real time and autonomously.
  • Data sovereignty regulations require precise control over where data flows.
  • Event-driven Architecture becomes more critical to enable business processes in real-time

An integration setup designed for yesterday simply cannot keep up with what the business needs today - and tomorrow.

 

4. What Enterprise-Wide Integration Consolidation Looks Like

Consolidating onto a single, enterprise-grade integration platform delivers benefits across the organization:

  • Unified visibility and control - One place to monitor, troubleshoot, and manage. When something goes wrong, teams know exactly where to look.
  • Streamlined governance - A single, standardized pipeline for promoting changes to production. Governance becomes something you can actually enforce consistently across all teams.
  • Increased agility - No more indecision about which tool to use. Teams move faster and focus on building value rather than managing infrastructure.
  • Cost optimization - Fewer platforms mean lower licensing costs, leaner operations, and people freed up for more innovative work.

Real-world numbers bring this to life. Murphy Oil consolidated multiple third-party iPaaS and SAP Process Orchestration onto SAP Integration Suite - migrating over 500 interfaces in around a year and achieving a 25% cost reduction for G&A spending. NuSkin saved $300,000 in the first year of consolidation alone, with ongoing annual savings reinvested into new initiatives. And Cona, a North American Coca-Cola bottler, cut its integration runtime costs by more than 50% while dramatically improving monitoring and error handling across its entire bottling ecosystem.

 

5. Why SAP Integration Suite Is the Right Platform to Consolidate On

SAP Integration Suite is built on open standards and designed to meet you where you are – no matter of how complex or diverse your landscape is. It brings together process integration, API management, B2B and B2G connectivity, event-driven integration, and AI agent support in one unified platform.

This breadth matters. Whether you are connecting business applications, exposing SAP S/4HANA data through APIs, managing EDI or e-invoicing, processing real-time events, or calling external AI models like Joule or Gemini as part of an integration flow - SAP Integration Suite handles it all without requiring a different tool for each scenario.

For the sixth consecutive year, Gartner has recognized SAP as a leader in the Integration Platform as a Service Magic Quadrant - citing comprehensive coverage of integration styles, deep AI capabilities, and the breadth of content on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With over 3,800 pre-built integration scenarios, 4,300 APIs for SAP and non-SAP systems, 600 business events for event-driven use cases, and connectors to over 500 top enterprise applications, the platform gives organizations a significant head start. Benchmarking with partners and customers has shown up to five times faster go-live and up to ten times lower build cost compared to starting from scratch.

6. Third-Party Connectivity – Reaching Every Corner of Your Landscape

 A key question when consolidating onto a single platform is whether it can actually connect to everything - not just SAP systems, but the full range of third-party applications, cloud services, legacy infrastructure, and partner ecosystems in a real enterprise landscape. SAP Integration Suite offers over 250 connectors out of the box, covering two distinct types.

Technology adapters work at the protocol level - HTTPS, SOAP, OData, SFTP, AMQP, JDBC, and many more. They are generic and reusable, giving developers full control over authentication and communication patterns. Any system that exposes a standard protocol can be reached this way.

Application-specific adapters go further. Purpose-built for individual business applications - Salesforce, Workday, Shopify, ServiceNow, and many others - they already know the target system's API structure and come with pre-configured operations. A developer simply selects what they need, and the adapter handles the rest. Less configuration, faster delivery, fewer errors.

An independent analysis of the top 516 enterprise applications confirmed that SAP Integration Suite has a technical or purpose-built adapter for every single one. Connectivity gaps that might justify keeping a secondary platform are, in practice, extremely rare.

But connectivity is only the starting point. What organizations also need is the business logic, data mapping, exception handling, and proven patterns that turn a system connection into a reliable, production-grade integration. This is where the third-party integration content on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub makes a real difference.

The Hub hosts over 3,800 pre-built integration scenarios for SAP and non-SAP systems - fully realized integration flows that come with end-to-end process logic, data transformations, error handling, security validation, and best practices from real-world deployments. This is not an open marketplace. Content is curated - published by SAP's engineering teams or by certified partners who are licensed to contribute and carry ongoing responsibility for keeping packages up to date. Every package comes with a component ID for direct support, and all content is fully customizable inside SAP Integration Suite.

 

7. Integration Strategy – Getting the Foundation Right

Consolidating onto a single platform is not just a technical exercise - it requires a clear integration strategy. As more workloads move onto one platform, traffic increases, processes become more business-critical, and expectations for reliability grow. Your strategy needs to account for this from the start.

SAP's Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M) provides exactly the structured foundation you need. It is a proven framework for mapping your integration landscape, categorizing use cases, and defining the right integration styles for each scenario - helping teams move from reactive, ad-hoc integration to a deliberate strategy that scales with the business.

Complementing ISA-M, SAP's integration assessment tooling helps organizations take stock of their existing estate - identifying what is running, what is redundant, what carries risk, and where consolidation opportunities exist. This is the essential starting point for a credible business case and a realistic migration roadmap.

Together, they give integration teams the language to have strategic conversations with business leadership - not just about technology, but about how integration enables agility, AI readiness, and long-term scalability.

 

8. Platform Operations – Running SAP Integration Suite at Enterprise Scale

A solid strategy sets the direction - but the operational foundation needs to be equally strong. When a single platform carries the full weight of enterprise integration, it becomes mission-critical infrastructure. How you build, deploy, monitor, and govern integrations matters more than ever.

The BTP Guidance Framework provides architectural and development best practices to ensure integrations are built consistently and securely from day one - and critically, these guidelines can be actively enforced through tooling, so standards are upheld as the platform grows.

For day-to-day operations, SAP Integration Suite provides built-in monitoring with real-time visibility into message flows and system health. This is extended further through SAP Cloud ALM, adding centralized monitoring, alerting, and health tracking across the full SAP landscape - a level of cross-landscape observability that simply cannot be achieved when monitoring is split across multiple platforms.

SAP Integration Suite also supports CI/CD pipelines and DevOps workflows, so integration teams can manage artifacts with the same discipline applied to application development - versioning, automated testing, and controlled deployment pipelines included. This is exactly what you need when the platform is running hundreds or thousands of business-critical integrations at once.

 

9. Building the Business Case for Consolidation

When making the case for consolidation internally, framing matters. Positioning it as a re-platforming project is difficult to sell - re-platforming in itself does not create business value. Positioning it around reducing risk, enabling AI, or delivering measurable cost savings is a much stronger conversation.

A few angles worth considering:

  • Integration debt reduction: Undocumented, poorly understood interfaces are a compliance and security risk. Consolidation is an opportunity to clean up the estate and reduce that exposure.
  • AI readiness: The agentic AI use cases that business leaders are asking about require clean data layers, purpose-built APIs, and real-time event-driven connectivity. Building that foundation now is what unlocks AI value later.
  • Governance and COE maturity: ISA-M gives integration teams a structured framework to get a clear picture of the current landscape and put the governance in place to operate at scale.
  • Hard cost savings: In many cases, the savings from shutting down redundant platforms and reallocating people are enough to justify the project on their own - as the Murphy Oil and NuSkin examples show.

The integration landscape is not getting simpler. AI agents, real-time event-driven architectures, composable ERP, and data sovereignty requirements are all adding new demands to what enterprise integration needs to deliver.

 

10. Join Our Webinar Series – See It in Action

Ready to go deeper? We have put together a webinar series that walks you through everything covered in this blog - live demos, real customer stories, and practical guidance from our product and solution experts. Whether you are just getting started or already planning your consolidation, there is a session for you.

 

  1. Rethinking Integration: Building a Unified Enterprise-wide Platform
  2. From Vision to Execution: Building a Scalable Integration Strategy
  3. Achieve Enterprise-grade Operations for SAP Integration Suite

 

11. What's Next? Here Is How to Get Started

Liked what you read? Below you will find everything you need to take the next step - from exploring the platform yourself to connecting with our team and diving into the content that is most relevant for your journey. We have classified the content into different personas – that means depending on your role and background you can select the fitting personas and get started at your own pace.

 

How to get started as Integration Strategist 

 

Asset Type

How is this asset useful?

Get started with the SAP Integration Suite website and learn more about iPaaS consolidation. Also, check out our Integration Roadmap to understand future capabilities and features.

New to SAP Integration Suite or just getting started? The product website gives you a solid platform overview, and the Roadmap Explorer shows you what is coming next -  so you can plan with confidence.

Whitepaper on Future-proof integration for cloud ERP  and SAP’s CIO Guide on Integration Architecture Guide

Need to bring leadership along? The Future-proof Integration whitepaper and CIO Guide give decision makers a concise, executive-level view of why modern integration architecture matters - and what to do about it.

Dive deeper with a demo video and our solution brief

See the platform in action and get the key facts in one place. Perfect for sharing with stakeholders who need a quick but concrete picture of what SAP Integration Suite can do.

Get started with the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology website and learn more about integration strategies in this blogpost: SAP Integration Solution Advisory at a Glance

Consolidation starts with a clear strategy. ISA-M gives you the structured methodology to map your landscape, categorize use cases, and define the right integration patterns - before you start building.

Get enabled on our official Learning Journey: Getting started with SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology to lay the right foundation and start developing with following mission: Get started with Integration Suite, Integration Assesment

Ready to dive deeper on Integration Strategy? The Learning Journey gets you up to speed on ISA-M, while the mission catalog and Integration Assessment help you take stock of your existing estate and build a realistic roadmap.

 

How to get started as Architect

 

Asset Type

How is this asset useful?

Get enabled on our official BTP Guidance framework to lay the right foundation and access more than 100 SAP Learning Journeys for SAP BTP and more

Build on the right foundation from day one. The BTP Guidance framework and Learning Journeys give you the architectural principles and hands-on enablement you need to design integrations that are secure, scalable, and built to last.

Navigate to the SAP Integration Suite service entry page in the SAP Discovery Center

Your one-stop shop for service details, pricing, and hands-on missions. The Discovery Center gives you everything you need to evaluate SAP Integration Suite.

Take a look at the marketecture of SAP BTP and explore SAP BTP reference architectures in the SAP Architecture Center. Here you can also review the BTP solution diagrams for detailed scenario setup.

Stop designing from scratch. The BTP reference architectures and solution diagrams give you proven, real-world blueprints you can adapt to your own landscape - saving time and reducing architectural risk from the start.

 

How to get started as Integration Developer

 

Asset Type

How is this asset useful?

Receive technical insights and documentation on SAP Integration Suite within the SAP Help Portal

Your go-to reference once you start building. When you hit a technical challenge, the SAP Help Portal has the documentation and guidance you need - with dedicated entries for both SAP Integration Suite.

Start developing: Check out the mission catalog and Get started with Integration Suite

The fastest way to get your hands dirty. The mission catalog walks you through real integration scenarios step by step, so you can go from zero to your first working integration in no time.

Develop your SAP skills for free, anywhere, anytime, and at your own pace on the  SAP Learning site

 

Keep your skills sharp at your own pace. The SAP Learning site gives you free access to structured courses and learning paths on SAP Integration Suite - whenever you need them.

Tune in to technology deep-dives in our Devtoberfest series

Go deep on the technical details. Devtoberfest sessions are built by developers, for developers - practical, hands-on, and focused on the topics that actually matter when you are building integrations.

Build and publish integration content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub and leverage published APIs & Events for development

Don't build from scratch when you don't have to. The Business Accelerator Hub gives you ready-to-use integration content, APIs, and events - so you can deliver faster and spend your time on what actually adds value.

 

How to get started as Operations Specialist

 

Asset Type

How is this asset useful?

Learn how to apply CI/CD for SAP Integration Suite  and review the technical documentation for CI/CD in SAP Integration Suite

Running a consolidated platform at scale means changes need to move through environments safely and consistently. CI/CD gives you the automation and pipeline discipline to do exactly that -  without breaking what is already working.

Learn about concepts of secure communication and apply the highest security standards Explore options for identity and access management in SAP Integration Suite.

When one platform carries all your enterprise integration workloads, security is non-negotiable. These resources help you apply the right standards and keep access tightly controlled from day one.

Run an integration flow under well defined boundary conditions and how to apply design guidelines

 

Good integrations don't just work - they are built to last. Applying design guidelines and running flows under well-defined boundary conditions ensures your integrations are stable, predictable, and easy to maintain at scale.

How to monitor integration flows with inbuilt monitoring capabilities and how to achieve centralized integration monitoring with Cloud ALM

You cannot manage what you cannot see. Built-in monitoring and Cloud ALM give you real-time visibility across all your integration flows - so you can catch issues early and keep business-critical processes running smoothly.

Enable Naming Conventions in integration development

Small things matter at scale. Consistent naming conventions across your integration estate make it dramatically easier to manage, troubleshoot, and hand over integrations - especially as your team and platform grow.

 

#StayTuned

entry.title: 🇵🇱 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Fri 22 May 2026 in Wrocław, PL, kindly hosted by Capgemini, thank you very much! Note that it takes place on the day before the SAP Inside Track Wrocław event which is on Sat 23 May 2026. 

⚠️ Registration for this CodeJam must be done on the SAP Essentials: SAP CodeJam - Hands-on with CAP CDS page which has been specially set up for this event.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

It seems the error is cached.

  2. Also, does the sql statement built in xsjs (classic) have the same length limitation as in sql console?

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP: Report generation with invalid weight in pac...
entry.content:
Hello,

RDP will mark erroneous items with report fraction "Calculated with data inconsistencies" and all weights and fees for them will be set to 0. The Packaging Composition will remain valid.

These items can be seen in Packaging Fees and Analyze Reporting Data applications. Further details for the data issues can be observed in the Data Quality app.

If the report output is generated in Run Compliance Reports application, the packaging composition will still be used because it is valid.

Best Regards,

Tsvetan

entry.title: Wrong behavior of sap.ui.core.theming.Parameters for sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartContainer
entry.content:
Hi,

After upgrading our system to S4/2023 and the latest available SAPUI5 ABAP Patch 1.120.39, i get this error from rendering the GanttChart we use in one of our views: [FUTURE FATAL] One or more parameters could not be found. - sap.ui.core.theming.Parameters. I debuged this and realized, that the hex color we use for the property "fill" in the sap.gantt.simple.BaseRectangle, will pass directly to the parameter sName from sap/ui/core/theming/Parameters.js and at the moment the method with the propertyName: sName is returned, the error appeare in the console. It seems, the difference between a real parameter name and a hex color can't be distinguished and therefore, i get too many errors, that i sent above, in the console.

Could you please help me to solve this?

 

Best regards,

Setareh Ganjimanesh

entry.title: Re: SAP SF Compensation - How to manage multiple Pay Comp...
entry.content:
Hi @SOFIAANNA_VAMVOUKLI 

Yes it's feasible.

To bring data into Compensation, either use a Pay Component Group or make sure every user has only one paycomponent flagged as "Use for Comp" = Comp in its settings.

To publish back to EC afterwards, you will need to hardcode everything because of these countries or legal entities for which you need to publish back to several paycomponents. If you look in the community you will lots of questions and how to on this.

But to answer your question yes its totally doable.

Thanks.

Xavier

entry.title: 1H 2026 SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: Enhanced I-9 and E-Verify Compliance Process
entry.content:
Introduction:

With the 1H 2026 release of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding, six meaningful enhancements have been delivered to the New Hire Details Page that give HR administrators greater control, cleaner data, and better compliance guardrails — without the need for Admin intervention or manual workarounds. This blog walks through each feature, why it matters, and how to activate it.

Feature 1: Cancel Form I-9 and E-Verify Processes

Enhancement Reason:

Once an I-9 or E-Verify process was initiated, there was no UI-based way to cancel it. Candidates who withdrew, roles that changed, and system-generated duplicates all produced "in-progress" records that could never be cleaned up without backend intervention. In high-volume hiring environments, this led to inflated compliance dashboards and real audit exposure.

What's New

  • HR administrators with the Manage I-9 Compliance Processes role-based permission can now cancel processes directly from the New Hire Details Page:
    • Compliance Processes Details → Cancel Process → Enter reason → Confirm
  • The system immediately updates the process status to CANCELLED, removes it from the active compliance queue, and logs the full action — who cancelled, when, and why — in the audit trail.

Cancellable statuses: INITIATED, IN PROGRESS

Cannot cancel: COMPLETED, CLOSED, CANCELLED

Key Pointers

  • Always provide a specific cancellation reason as it helps anyone who is validating the audit trail.
  • Do not cancel after an E-Verify case has been submitted to DHS — contact E-Verify support first
  • Cancellation is permanent — if done in error, a new I-9 process must be created from scratch

Feature 2: Initiate I-9 Correction or Reverification from the New Hire Details Page

Enhancement Reason:

Triggering an I-9 correction or reverification previously required navigating away from the new hire context or raising an Admin request. This delayed corrections and created gaps in audit trails.

What's New

The Initiate I-9 Process button on the New Hire Details Page opens a dialog that lets HR select the process type and provide a reason. The system then:

  1. Validates that the I-9 feature is enabled and the user has eligibility
  2. Checks that no open I-9 compliance process already exists (duplicate prevention)
  3. Creates a new Compliance Process record of the selected type
  4. Automatically starts the corresponding i9 workflow

Supported trigger types:

  • CORRECT I-9 SECTION 1, CORRECT I-9 SECTION 2— correct errors in employee or employer sections
  • INITIATE REVERIFICATION — re-verify employment authorization when documents expire

Feature 3: Citizenship Status Change During Form I-9 Reverification

Enhancement Reason:

Employee immigration status changes over time — a permanent resident may naturalize, a visa holder may adjust status. Traditional reverification workflows did not accommodate this, forcing HR to create entirely new I-9 forms and introducing data inconsistencies between I-9 records and E-Verify.

What's New

  • During Section 3 completion (reverification), employees can now update their citizenship status.
  • The Citizenship Status model uses E-Verify API standard codes (US_CITIZEN, LAWFUL_PERMANENT_RESIDENT, ALIEN_AUTHORIZED_TO_WORK), and the I9 To EVerify Data Converter in the backend will ensure that the updated status is correctly mapped and synchronized with E-Verify automatically.
  • Historical citizenship status is preserved in the audit trail for both I-9 compliance and E-Verify continuity.

Important Notes

  • Status updates must be supported by appropriate documentation
  • Changes trigger a new E-Verify case creation
  • Retain copies of both old and new documentation

Feature 4: Enhanced TNC Instructions for E-Verify Manual Onboarding

Enhancement Reason:

A Tentative Non-Confirmation (TNC) from E-Verify indicates a mismatch between employee-provided data and government records. Without structured guidance, HR teams struggled to explain next steps to employees, leading to confusion, potential risk, and missed regulatory deadlines.

What's New

When E-Verify returns a TNC status, the system now automatically triggers E-Verify FAN Workflow in the backend, which:

  • Generates a Further Action Notice (FAN) document with the TNC reason, employee rights, SSA/DHS contact information, and the 8-federal-working-day response deadline
  • Sends multi-channel notifications to the employee
  • Manages electronic signature of the FAN acknowledgment
  • Sends automated alerts to managers when the employee takes action
  • Provides real-time case status in the onboarding dashboard

For manual onboarding scenarios (where automated E-Verify submission fails), HR can use the enhanced Create Manual E-Verify to initiate cases manually and receive the same guided TNC resolution workflow.

Feature 5: Configurable Trigger for I-9 Reverification

Enhancement Reason:

Manually tracking document expiration dates and initiating reverifications was not easy as it was not configurable. Missed reverifications create compliance gaps; Organizations needed both automation and flexibility.

What's New

  • The Trigger I9 Reverification Job automatically identifies employees with documents expiring within a configurable threshold, validates eligibility (completed I-9, no open process), and creates a Compliance Process of type I9 REVERIFICATION with the full workflow and notifications.
  • HR can also manually trigger reverification at any time from the employee profile or New Hire Details Page via Actions → Trigger I-9 Reverification.
  • You can configure reverification trigger between 30 - 540 days, from the compliance settings page under i-9 settings

Feature 6: Form I-9 Guardrail for Receipt Documents in E-Verify

Enhancement Reason:

When employees cannot present original I-9 documents at hire, they may present receipt documents — temporary proof that they have applied for replacement documents. These receipts are valid for only 90 days (8 days for government-issued receipts like I-94), and E-Verify cases cannot be submitted using receipt documents. Without automated detection, HR teams frequently missed these constraints.

What's New

  • The i9 receipt service automatically detects when a receipt document is selected during Section 2 completion.
  • When triggered, the system displays a guardrail message to both the employee and HR with clear instructions.

entry.title: Your Innovation Boost: A Shot of SAP at Hannover Messe
entry.content:
What have ginger shots and SAP in common? 

You have no idea? Then let’s find out! 

Every year in April, all eyes turn to Hannover Messe, a place buzzing with new ideas, groundbreaking innovations, and of course, AI!  

This year marked my very first time attending and honestly, nothing could have prepared me for what I walked into. The sheer scale of it left me speechless. We’re talking about massive booths that don’t just display products but tell entire stories through their complexity and stunning visual design. The look and feel of everything around me was nothing short of spectacular, a perfect blend of cutting-edge technology and creative presentation.
Imagine crowded halls, enormous booths showcasing the latest in robotics and technology, all brought together in one breathtaking area.

And right in the middle of it all, you’ll find SAP!

The Vibe of the World’s Industrial Heartbeat  

Hannover Messe is more than just a trade fair; it’s the epicentre of industrial transformation. The energy is electric. Everywhere you look, you see passion and progress. This year’s theme, "Think Tech Forward," perfectly captures that feeling. It’s all about looking ahead and embracing the technologies that will shape the future of industry. 
 
The main buzz was around how digitalization, AI, and sustainability can drive real change. The focus was on practical solutions that help companies stay competitive while also protecting our planet. Artificial Intelligence was a huge topic, especially how it’s being woven directly into production with autonomous robots and Generative AI. 
And walking through the halls, you could actually feel that vision coming to life. 

 

Our Role in the Digital Factory 

And SAP? We’re the digital thread that weaves through it all. While amazing machines and robots were on display everywhere, we provide the intelligent software that connects the entire value chain, from design to operations, to build the resilient and sustainable businesses of the future. 

The SAP booth this year was less "corporate exhibition stand" and more "place you accidentally spend two hours at". There was a lot to see, a lot to try, and something worth sticking around for at the very end. 

 

Highlights from the SAP Booth 

AI That Actually Makes Sense 

Let’s be honest, AI is everywhere right now. But at the SAP booth, it wasn’t just a buzzword on a slide. The demos showed how solutions like SAP IBP and SAP S/4HANA are using embedded AI to genuinely make supply chains smarter through better forecasting, sharper inventory planning, and faster reactions to disruptions. 

Less "AI for the sake of AI." More "AI that actually helps you sleep at night." 

Don’t just take our word for it, it was fantastic to see our customer, SMA Solar Technology, on stage explaining how they automate their planning processes with SAP Integrated Business Planning.  You want to know more about it? Just follow this link.

Knowing What’s Going On, in real time 

Supply chain visibility was another major theme, and for good reason. Nobody wants surprises anymore, at least not the kind that involve empty shelves or delayed shipments. SAP demonstrated how its solutions give companies a real-time view across their entire network, enabling them to spot problems early and react before they escalate.  

It’s the kind of capability that sounds simple but changes everything. 

 

So, What About That Ginger Shot? 

Now, back to our initial question. The final product coming off our high-tech, digitally connected production line was a freshly bottled ginger shot, ready to take with you! 
It might sound like a small gimmick, but it was the perfect metaphor. 

Because the whole SAP booth experience was built exactly like a well-run supply chain. Every step connected, every touchpoint intentional, everything building toward something. You moved through planning, visibility, sustainability, AI. Each piece linking to the next. And then, right at the end of the chain, a physical reward was waiting. 

So, a visit to our booth didn’t just give you a metaphorical boost of innovation; you could walk away with a real one. 

More information about the Story of a Ginger Shot and of course about SAP IBP.

 

See You Next Year! 

Hannover Messe 2026 was a lot.  
A lot of inspiration, a lot of conversations, a lot of "wait, can you show me that again?" moments. And a surprisingly good ginger shot. If you were there, you know the feeling. If not, there’s always next year.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Development Newsletter issue #40 (May 2026)
entry.content:
The mid-year conference season is upon us with many opportunities for you to meet our development teams and have conversations with your peers about the topics you care about most. Some of our upcoming events include:

  • SAP Sapphire in Orlando on May 11-13
  • SAP UX Innovation Day in Palo Alto on June 11
  • UI5con in St. Leon-Rot on July 14

Join us to learn about using AI to accelerate development, MCP servers, and the latest UX strategy updates from SAP

If you want to join other developers to discuss various traditional and AI-powered Fiori development-related topics from the comfort of your office or home, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable. Register online or email me for an invitation.

Development News

SAP Fiori elements feature map: what is available in each SAPUI5 version

If you work with SAP Fiori elements, you have probably run into the problem where it is hard to see in which UI5 version a specific feature was introduced. Marian Zeis created a feature map that shows when a feature first appears. It’s updated automatically whenever new versions are released. Explore the feature map.

Key User Adaptation: powerful new features and seamless transition to Developer Extensibility

With key user adaptation, you can change the user interface of SAP Fiori apps directly in the Fiori launchpad. The goal of key user extensibility is to empower business users to be able to adapt their working environment to suit their needs, without having to rely on system administrators. Learn more.

SAP Fiori tools 2603 update: RAP Project Accelerator, walkthrough, adaptation improvements, and more

SAP Fiori tools 2603 delivers improvements across development transparency, onboarding, AI-supported development, and extensibility. Highlights include Project Accelerator support for RAP, a change in navigation from properties to its source, a new walkthrough in Visual Studio Code, and the ability to import existing key user changes into an SAPUI5 Adaptation Project. See details.

Introducing UI5 plugins for Claude: AI-powered development for SAPUI5 and OpenUI5

UI5 plugins for Claude enhance UI5 development workflows with AI assistance by embedding current, maintained guidelines directly into the development workflow. The result is that Claude Code provides UI5-accurate suggestions without you having to paste documentation into every conversation. Learn more.

How to migrate UI5 projects to the latest UI5 best practices

This blog post focuses on how UI5 MCP server can support you in migrating your UI5 project easily to the latest best practices with the help of GitHub Copilot Coding Agent. Migrate your project.

Declarative UI Integration Cards - A consistent framework with flexible content

UI Integration Cards are utilized in many SAP solutions. A new blog post shows key qualities of declarative Integration Cards: They offer a range of display options and minimalist interactions, including List Card, Table Card, Timeline, Calendar, Analytical, and Object Card with input elements. Read more.

More power, new name: The UI Flexibility Service explained

SAPUI5 flexibility allows developers, key users, and end users to adapt standard apps with simple drag-and-drop tools. Since it’s not only for key users, we renamed this capability to UI flexibility service instead of the older, potentially confusing name UI5 flexibility for key users. Learn more.

Navigating from an SAP Fiori elements application to another application

While the SAPUI5 documentation explains how to navigate to other applications in detail, the code snippets sometimes make it difficult to visualize how each navigation pattern behaves in the UI. Mio Yasutake created a sample repository that demonstrates various Fiori elements navigation patterns along with their actual UI behavior. See how.

Events

SAP UX Innovation Days are back for 2026

SAP UX Innovation Day Silicon Valley – Thursday, June 11

Join us at SAP UX Innovation Day Silicon Valley to learn about the intersection between AI and user experience. Our goal is to empower you with knowledge on how to build SAP Fiori apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and all the new AI functionality that we are building into the SAP Fiori elements framework. We will cover AI for business users and AI-powered tools for application developers.

Register and more details.

Mark your calendar for other SAP UX Innovation Days in your region:

Dublin, Ireland - Wednesday, September 30

Walldorf, Germany - Thursday, December 10

Other Events

Sapphire – lots of great UX sessions

UI5con - A vibrant gathering for UI5 enthusiasts to connect, learn, and innovate

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori Development newsletter March 2026 (issue #39)

SAP Fiori development newsletter January 2026 (issue #38)

SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2025 (issue #37)

SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2025 (issue #36)

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2025 (issue #35)

SAP FIori development newsletter May 2025 (Issue #34)

entry.title: How has RISE with SAP changed ownership across teams in your organization?
entry.content:
Since moving to RISE with SAP, things kind of shifted around. Stuff like infrastructure isn't really ours anymore as SAP handles most of that. We've been in the process of figuring out what we actually own.

We’re less involved in the technical operations and more focused on integration and making processes work across the business. At the beginning it wasn’t always clear who owned what, but over time it’s become more defined.

Curious to hear how others are navigating that shift, has it made things simpler or just different?

entry.title: ‌🇵🇱‌ Webinar: Implement Poland SAF-T with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Join us for the virtual session Implement Poland SAF-T with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - A Practical Walkthrough.

In this webinar, SAP experts will guide you through the recommended migration path from the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T, including the role and timeline of the Compatibility Pack, to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance as SAP’s strategic solution for Polish tax and statutory reporting.

The session explains how SAP Document and Reporting Compliance supports Polish localization requirements by delivering comprehensive JPK reporting structures, aligned with current statutory obligations. In addition, you will receive an overview of the solution’s KSeF e‑invoicing capabilities, supporting compliance with mandatory electronic invoice exchange in Poland.

Learn more and register now!

If you want to get more information about the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in Poland provided by SAP experts, join the Poland - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Work Zone group.
